title: Subitems in Views and Automations etc
excerpt:
Summary: 
Subitems need the increased functionality of main Items to realize their full potential. 
Detail:

posts.author: GUM (Ryan at GUM)
posts.cookedHtml:
Summary:
Subitems need the increased functionality of main Items to realize their full potential.

Detail:
I’d like to set up different Views beyond the Main Table View for a board that uses Subitems but they can’t be accessed. Only Items in Item Groups appear available for viewing.

I’d also like to set up Notification Automations for a board with subitems but, again, the available Automations only reference the main Items in Item Groups, not the Subitems under Items. For example, a People column and Date column within a Subitem cannot be linked from the Automations menu and therefore no Notification Automation can be created for the tasks I have built into Subitems.

Is there a plan to more fully integrate Subitems into the functionality available to primary Items?

Having more options to use Subitems as another functional layer of hierarchy under Items would dramatically improve their usefulness. Thanks in advance for considering.

posts.author: melissav-monday.com (Melissa - monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @GUM - thanks for sharing these details about your workflow! I definitely understand how increased subitem functionality would be helpful in these situations.

Support for subitems within the different board views and automations are both in planning, so we hope to roll out these out by the end of Q3. You can find our full subitem road map here: Subitem Product Plan

posts.author: GUM (Ryan at GUM)
posts.cookedHtml: Great, thanks for sharing that roadmap. Looking forward to updates

posts.author: Chang (Chang-Ching)
posts.cookedHtml: Subitems are quite helpful and greatly expand the possibility. Looking forwards to the new updates!

posts.author: tina (Tina)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi Monday team, I was following this thread and noticed on the product roadmap, automations on subitems was due for Q3 2020 - any update to the plan here? Thanks!

posts.author: Bibi (Bibi)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Monday,
I am also interested in this. Please let us know. Thank you!

posts.author: robgibbs (Rob Gibbs)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @melissav-monday.com

Thanks for this… let me add to this thread with a specific automation request…

When {SubitemDate} arrives/passes change {SubItem Status} to //Status//

Right now it looks like you can’t access the subitems status in the automation.

THANKS
Rob

posts.author: andrewalmand (Andrew Almand)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @robgibbs

This automation should be available. I was able to get both the subitem date and status to show up in the Custom Automation:

image1365×270 30.6 KB

If its still not working for you, maybe contact support to see if they can troubleshoot.

posts.author: robgibbs (Rob Gibbs)
posts.cookedHtml:
Yes…that one is on me…I needed to better understand the details of creating these…It doesn’t do “When SubItem Date Passes set Subitem status to something”…

I didn’t realize you could do it When Date Arrives and set when to 5pm so effectively it doesn’t do anything until the end of the business day…

Just kind of have to play around with the logic that you use to find the path through to what you are trying to accomplish…

THANKS
Rob
